title: Tide Pool Reserve: Intertidal Habitat Delineation
strategies: 1x1 baseline: draw a unit cell around each organism's survey point -- feasible but tiny, reproduces the evaluator's weak reference and scores ~0.1

single-pass greedy growth in input order: grow each pool one side at a time toward its target area, taking the largest feasible non-overshooting, non-overlapping extension; early organisms starve later ones

construct-then-refine: grow small-target pools first, then a seeded local search that grows/shrinks random sides accepting non-worsening moves, so overshooting pools free space that neighbours reclaim
infeasible: delineate every pool as the whole shore -> overlapping interiors -> the layout is rejected and scores 0
